key_insight: Casing eccentricity and weak cement-formation interfaces strongly alter fracture initiation pressure and crack paths in heterogeneous wellbore systems, with eccentricity lowering initiation pressure and weak interfaces diverting cracks along the interface.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies geometric and interfacial conditions that can trigger earlier, more complex wellbore failure, informing safer casing design and cement integrity assessment in drilling and production.
method: Hybrid phase-field fracture model for heterogeneous wellbore systems with weak interfaces
Benchmark validation against analytical and numerical reference problems
Parametric simulations varying casing eccentricity, interface strength, and 3D geometry
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on benchmark cases and numerical experiments showing up to 30% lower crack-initiation pressure, interface-deflected cracks at 30% interface strength, and 3D depth-dependent crack nucleation effects.
watch_item: The most complex crack interactions are captured numerically, so predictive accuracy will depend on mesh resolution, phase-field regularization choices, and how well material/interface properties are calibrated.
paper_title: Phase-field analysis of fracture in heterogeneous wellbore systems: effects of casing eccentricity and cement-formation interface strength
